Always a fun place to visit.  I had gone several times as a teenager and even did a class report on it.  We did a Mansion Tour and a Basement and Attic tour.  Our guides have always been great.  Only one was a little boring.  The Basement and Attic tours are on your own with arrows and signs directing your way.  They have a very large gift shop. I didn't get more pictures as I had been there before.

It is a wonderful step back in time. The part that everyone talks about is the ghost stuff but honestly we didn't go for that aspect. The story of the home and the architecture/history was like stepping back in time. We really enjoyed it. The home is quirky as everyone knows but when you look at where it sits in the heart of the city now it's such a  fun contrast.
Also the basement and attic tour were priced well and we enjoyed that too. It was kind of disappointing that they play weird/creepy music to make the basement seem creepier. I felt it distracted from the experience, especially if you have ever been in an old home.
It was fun and the tour guides are very knowledgeable.
